subject: Update APPC resources for Nitrogen ODL
commit_message:
Update APPC resources for Nitrogen ODL

Updated installation scripts based on changes to ccsdk.
Updated properties file to support Nitrogen ODL.

Change-Id: Ib714fe0fc4a2adec839dc573700338db4129eeb5
Issue-ID: OOM-1021
Signed-off-by: Aaron Hay <ah415j@att.com>

entries.message:

Also, I see the following bundles status:

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

Missed issue in kubernetes/onap/values.yaml that Borislav identified.  Those values need to be moved to the appc section.

Patch Set 3:

(1 comment)

What about the failing bundles?

This issue was caused by the order of installation of the clustering bundles. After shifting the order of installation for the clustering bundles, I no longer see the sixteen bundles failing.

> > (1 comment)

 > Karaf :: Diagnostic :: Boot
 > 55 | Resolved |  80 | 2.1.0.SNAPSHOT                      |
 > aaf-shiro-aafrealm-osgi-bundle, Hosts: 130
 > 190 | Resolved |  80 | 0.7.1                               |
 > config-persister-directory-xml-adapter, Hosts: 192
 > 191 | Resolved |  80 | 0.7.1                               |
 > config-persister-file-xml-adapter, Hosts: 192
 > 421 | Failure  |  80 | 1.3.0.SNAPSHOT                      | APPC
 > Command Executor - Core
 > Is this expected?
 > 
 > Also, I noticed that it took more than 1/2 hour for appc to start.
 > I opened a bug for this: https://jira.onap.org/browse/APPC-874

I believe you see the long delay and the failure because you kept the default configuration information for OpenStack. If you change the defaults to valid values, then you should see the results I posted without a large delay as well.

appc:
  enabled: true
  config:
    openStackType: OpenStackProvider
    openStackName: OpenStack
    openStackKeyStoneUrl: http://1.2.3.4:5000/v3
    openStackServiceTenantName: APPC
    openStackDomain: Default
    openStackUserName: username
    openStackEncryptedPassword: password
